reasoning: The matching condition was too broad (`matched_uv is None or len(matched_uv) == 0`) and incorrectly skipped edge matching. The fix adds `substitution_possible = M and N` and gates the branch with `at_least_one_node_match and substitution_possible`, correcting decision logic.

Exception formatting incorrectly preferred `value.msg` for non-SyntaxError exceptions due broad try/except flow. The patch switches to an `else` branch using `self._some_str(value)`, so `.msg` is no longer wrongly prioritized.

TypedDict context resolution treated partial union matches as definitive and emitted ambiguity errors even when context was non-exhaustive. The fix tracks an `exhaustive` flag from `find_typeddict_context` and only applies strict/ambiguous TypedDict handling when exhaustive is true.
Statistics calculations divided by `self.count[i]` without guarding zero counts, causing `ZeroDivisionError`. The patch adds `if self.count[i] else 0` guards in `mean`, `rms`, and `var`.
`forget()` cleared backend data but missed the pending-result cleanup path, leaving stale pending entries. The added `self.backend.remove_pending_result(self)` implements the missing cleanup handler.

Boolean-op branch typing used only `expanded_left_type` as context for the right operand, producing incorrect inference in union/literal cases. The patch introduces `_combined_context(...)` and passes the merged context into `analyze_cond_branch`.

Constraint inference used `eager=False` unconditionally when combining union-item constraints, which led to wrong `Never` inference for `Optional[Any]`. The fix makes eagerness conditional (`eager=isinstance(actual, AnyType)`) to choose constraints correctly.

A rare partition-mismatch case triggered an assertion instead of being handled gracefully. The patch replaces the assert with an `if not all(...): return [], cosets`, adding the missing edge-case handler.

Option precedence was integrated in the wrong order: machine-file options were applied before project defaults, so native-file `buildtype` could be ignored. The fix reorders initialization to apply machine-file options after project defaults.
`ValidationError` had no string rendering path, so tracebacks lacked useful details. The patch adds `__str__` that iterates failed authorizations/challenges and formats their errors.
Circuit diagram generation had no branch for zero-qubit `MatrixGate`, causing downstream diagram validation failure. The fix adds `if n_qubits == 0: return NotImplemented` to handle that scenario.

Type compatibility checks handled `Literal` only on the superclass side, missing the case where the subclass itself is a `Literal[...]`. The new `if is_literal(possible_subclass)` branch validates each literal value against the target superclass type.

Filename sanitization used a raw string for `removed_chars`, so `'0'` was treated as removable instead of only the NUL escape sequence behavior intended. The patch corrects the character literal and also fixes window-title state retrieval to use `_window_title`, restoring correct filename derivation.

Leading-indent normalization removed whitespace based only on the first line and failed for consistently indented pasted blocks with comments. The fix replaces that custom slicing logic with `textwrap.dedent(...)` for proper common-indent handling.

CRS metadata serialization called `crs.to_json()`, producing a JSON string nested inside JSON instead of an object. The patch switches to `crs.to_json_dict()`, which is the correct API for object-typed serialization.

Week/year formatting logic computed boundary weeks incorrectly for locale-specific rules (e.g., end-of-year week rollover). The patch rewrites week-of-year/month calculations and adjusts `format_year` around Jan/Dec boundaries using new `get_week_of_year`/`get_week_of_month` helpers.

Numeric comparison operators built expressions from `self` instead of normalized `+self`, causing wrong JS output/coercion behavior in conditions. The fix consistently uses `+self` across comparison operations and removes conflicting boolean-conversion logic.

The fix removes the generic `hasattr(var, "__len__")` path and only calls `len()` for known sequence types, avoiding misuse of type objects that expose `__len__` but are not valid length-bearing instances. This is a type-handling/API usage correction in `whos` display logic.

The diff call is reconfigured with `with_unknown=with_missing` and `with_unchanged=with_missing` so cache-missing objects are surfaced correctly. The bug was caused by wrong diff option wiring between components.

The transformer previously always dumped held flips into measurements, which broke mid-circuit measurement semantics. The patch removes the measurement-absorption handler path (`_dump_into_measurement`) to avoid applying an unsupported scenario.

Boolean composition operators now include `empty` in generated predicate dicts for both sides when parameters are selections. The previous implementation lacked handling to preserve `Parameter.empty` through `&`, `|`, and `~`.
The code now guards against empty `task_id` by creating `body_task_id = task_id or uuid()` before freezing the body. This adds the missing validation/fallback for an empty identifier case.

The patch detects pytest fixture wrappers (`__pytest_wrapped__` and `_fixture_function`) and wraps the underlying callable instead of replacing the fixture object. This fixes incompatible API handling across pytest versions.
The uniqueness constraint inclusion condition changed from `set(field_names)` to `model_fields_names`, correcting which set is checked for superset coverage. This is a wrong-condition logic fix.
`handles` is converted to `list(handles)` before `len(handles)` is used, so iterables like `itertools.chain` work. The failure came from assuming a sized sequence API on a generic iterator.

The matcher constructors now explicitly validate directedness compatibility and raise errors for wrong matcher/graph combinations. The original bug came from missing guard checks on graph type constraints.

The fix initializes `current_layer` from `visited` after materializing sources, preventing generator re-consumption issues that duplicated source nodes. This corrects iterable/generator handling semantics.
Verbose environment messages are redirected from `io.write_line` to `io.write_error_line`, separating logs from command output used by `eval`. This is an output-channel integration/configuration fix.
`remote list` now handles the default-remote case by adding a `(default)` marker and style when `name == default_remote`. The prior code lacked a branch for this specific scenario.
`open_datatree` now passes `backend.open_dataset_parameters` into `_resolve_decoders_kwargs` instead of an empty tuple. The issue was incorrect backend-decoder parameter wiring.

Call nesting tracking changed from an integer sentinel to a stack of call line numbers, and related conditions were updated (`not call_stack`, `len(call_stack) == 1`). This fixes flawed parsing logic that dropped translator comments for multiline strings.

A fallback path was added to resolve custom functions serialized without `package>name` by matching suffixes in `custom_objects`. The bug was a missing deserialization handler for legacy serialized names.

`status` assignment now normalizes through `enum.get_member(constants.ChatMemberStatus, status, status)` instead of storing raw strings. This is a type/API handling correction for enum-backed status values.
Axes now stores `args[0].frozen()` when initialized from a `Bbox`, preventing shared mutable reference side effects. The bug stemmed from misuse of object/reference semantics for the position API.

The fix removes the NumPy-only unitary validation branch (`linalg.is_unitary`) so `cirq.unitary` behavior is consistent across strategies. This is a compatibility/integration inconsistency between code paths rather than a new algorithm.

`UninhabitedType.__hash__` and `__eq__` previously ignored the `ambiguous` flag, causing distinct types to collapse in caches. The patch includes `self.ambiguous` in both methods, correcting equality/hash logic.
`__getattribute__` did not handle `protocol` as a class-level forwarded attribute for cached filesystems. Adding `"protocol"` to the handled set adds the missing branch for this scenario.
Deleting an EXIF tag removed it from `_data` but left matching entries in `_ifds`, so GPS-specific deletion was incomplete. The fix adds the missing `_ifds` deletion path when that tag exists.

On Unix, the function returned `/var/tmp/...` before honoring `DVC_SITE_CACHE_DIR`, so env configuration was effectively ignored. The patch moves env handling to an early return, fixing config precedence/integration.
The client was missing a guard against querying RenewalInfo for expired certificates. The new `if cert.not_valid_after_utc < now` early return enforces the required expiration check.
Subfigure axes were reprocessed at parent level, compounding margin adjustments and shrinking layouts. The fix tracks `axsdone` and filters those axes out, correcting traversal logic.

Stubgen inferred `None` defaults as `_typeshed.Incomplete | None`, producing incorrect type output. The patch changes `get_str_type_of_node` options and call sites to avoid incomplete-type insertion for this case.

`add_options()` called `_update_lines()` before ensuring layout dimensions were refreshed, so mounted empty lists could stay invisible. Adding `refresh(layout=...)` fixes the update-order integration issue.

`commit()` did not properly handle the cross-device `PermissionError` path from `shutil.move` fallback behavior. The patch adds explicit exception handling and conditional re-raise, covering the missing error-handling scenario.

`Path.interpolated()` effectively assumed simple line segments and failed on paths with `MOVETO` components or `CLOSEPOLY` edge cases. The patch adds dedicated handling for connected components and closepoly vertices, filling missing path-type handlers.

HLS parsing lacked handling for audio renditions without full codec/bitrate metadata, especially alternate audio groups. The patch adds audio/alternate detection and preference metadata (`source_preference`, `_audio_group_id`) to handle that scenario.
Reconstruction functions assumed offsets started at zero and did not support sliced ragged arrays. The added `coords = coords[offsets[0]:]` branch introduces the missing sliced-input handling.

Offset arrays were emitted as default int64, causing type compatibility issues with int32-based GeoArrow list offsets. The patch introduces dtype selection and centralized offset construction to produce the correct offset type.

Using `stream.readuntil(b"\n")` forced newline-terminated reads, preventing partial line flushes from appearing live. Switching to `stream.read(100)` uses the stream API appropriately for incremental output.

Mask shape validation did not handle TensorFlow graph-mode/partial-batch behavior and attempted invalid comparisons. The patch adds a backend-specific branch for TF graph mode, providing the missing runtime-specific handler.
